title: Jet Reconstruction (Anti-kT)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Jet Reconstruction (Anti-kT): Jet reconstruction: cluster final-state particles into jets using anti-kT algorithm with pileup subtraction and JES calibration. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.antikt.sequential_clustering…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; pileup_contamination_residual, underlying_event_modeling set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: d_ij = min(k_{Ti}^{-2}, k_{Tj}^{-2}) * Delta_ij ** 2 / R ** 2; jet E corrected by JES(eta, pt, mu)
E.world_state_x: jet 4-vectors {E, px, py, pz} for N_jets jets above pt threshold
E.observation_y: calorimeter clusters + track momenta for N_particles per event

W.regime: Existence of the recovered jet_4vector_list is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); pileup_contamination_residual dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Pileup detector gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: JER < 5% for pt > 100 GeV; JES uncertainty < 1%. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |pileup_contamination_residual|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_particles ** 2) naive, O(N_particles * log N_particles) FastJet per iteration
